studyName: Subpopulations and Intermediate Outcome Measures in COPD Study (SPIROMICS)

tables.description: The longitudinal phenotype dataset contains subject IDs and a core set of variables of interest from SPIROMICS visits baseline through visit 4.

variables.name: ACLIDINIUM
variables.description: Inhaled bronchodilator used in the last 3 months: aclidinium (Tudorza) at Visit includes Other inhaled steroids or bronchodilators

variables.name: AGE_DERV
variables.description: Age in years at visit based on date of birth and date of visit

variables.name: ALBUTEROL
variables.description: Inhaled bronchodilators used in last 3 months: albuterol (Proventil, Ventolin, ProAir) at Visit includes Other inhaled steroids or bronchodilators

variables.name: ALBUTEROLIPRATROPIUMBROMID
variables.description: Nebulized bronchodilators used in last 3 months: albuterol and ipratropium bromide (DuoNeb) at Visit includes Other inhaled steroids or bronchodilators

variables.name: ALBUTEROL_NEB
variables.description: Nebulized bronchodilators used in last 3 months: albuterol (Proventil, Ventolin, ProAir) at Visit includes Other inhaled steroids or bronchodilators

variables.name: APNEA_DIAGNOSED
variables.description: Sleep apnea diagnosed by a health professional reported

variables.name: ARFORMOTEROL
variables.description: Nebulized bronchodilators used in last 3 months: arformoterol (Brovana) includes Other inhaled steroids or bronchodilators

variables.name: AVERAGE_PACKS_PER_DAY
variables.description: Average number of packs smoked per day

variables.name: AVGDIA
variables.description: Average Diastolic Blood Pressure

variables.description: Average Heart Rate

variables.name: AVGSYS
variables.description: Average Systolic Blood Pressure

variables.description: Body Mass Index

variables.name: BSQ_APNEARISK

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
